topic: Databricks Managed MCP Servers: Developer Setup and Unity Catalog Integration Guide

key_points: Official Databricks docs for managed MCP servers on AWS
Covers OAuth setup, Python 3.12+ requirements, and dependency installation (mcp>=1.9, databricks-sdk[openai], mlflow>=3.1.0, databricks-agents>=1.0.0, databricks-mcp)
Shows how to view MCP server endpoint URLs via workspace > Agents > MCP Servers
Explains DatabricksMCPClient().get_databricks_resources() for resource retrieval
Covers on-behalf-of-user auth and Unity Catalog permission enforcement
Official announcement of managed MCP servers with Unity Catalog and Mosaic AI
Three managed server types: Genie (structured data NL queries), Vector Search (RAG retrieval), UC Functions (predefined SQL queries)
MCP Catalog (Beta) for discovery and governance
AI Gateway as central management hub for MCP access monitoring
Launch partners include S&P Global, Factset, Glean, Dun and Bradstreet, Moody's
Practical guide for building custom MCP servers on Databricks Apps
Uses FastAPI and FastMCP pattern for custom server hosting
Shows how to extend managed MCP with domain-specific tools
Covers deployment via Databricks Apps as a serverless hosting option
Includes testing and validation steps
OpenAI Cookbook: Building supply-chain copilot with OpenAI Agent SDK and Databricks MCP
Demonstrates cross-vendor compatibility (OpenAI SDK consuming Databricks MCP servers)
Shows real-world enterprise use case (supply chain)
Explains how MCP enables vendor-agnostic agent tool integrations
Covers authentication between OpenAI agents and Databricks endpoints
Independent analysis: hype vs real transformation of MCPs on Databricks
Discusses operational overhead of self-hosted MCPs at scale
Notes that hundreds of agents using centralized MCP requires dedicated ops support
Evaluates Databricks managed approach vs DIY self-hosted alternatives
Raises cost and benefit considerations for enterprise teams

stats.fact: MCP SDK monthly downloads reached 97 million by March 2026 — a 970x increase in 18 months from 100,000 downloads at launch in November 2024
17,468 MCP servers indexed across registries as of Q1 2026 (Nerq independent census)
Remote MCP servers grew nearly 4x since May 2025, demonstrating strong enterprise interest in production deployments
85% of enterprises expected to implement AI agents by end of 2025
MCP market projected to reach $1.8 billion in 2025, driven by healthcare, finance, and manufacturing demand
By 2026, 75% of API gateway vendors and 50% of iPaaS vendors will have MCP features
Databricks managed MCP servers enforce a limit of 20 tools across all connected servers

angles: Zero-to-working in under 15 minutes: step-by-step quickstart using OAuth and the databricks-mcp Python library
Unity Catalog governance as a differentiator: why Databricks managed approach beats self-hosted MCPs for enterprise
Choosing the right managed server type: Genie vs Vector Search vs UC Functions for different data access patterns
MCP Catalog and AI Gateway: how to discover, govern, and monitor MCP tools at organizational scale
Cross-vendor compatibility: using Databricks MCP servers with OpenAI, Anthropic, and other agent frameworks
Cost of ownership analysis: managed Databricks MCP vs self-hosted vs cloud-hosted alternatives
outline: H2: What Are Databricks Managed MCP Servers? (Overview and server types: Genie, Vector Search, UC Functions)
H2: Prerequisites and Environment Setup (Python 3.12+, pip dependencies, Databricks CLI)
H2: Step 1 - Authenticate to Your Workspace with OAuth (U2M OAuth flow, CLI login, profile configuration)
H2: Step 2 - Locate Your MCP Server Endpoints (Workspace > Agents > MCP Servers, endpoint URL retrieval)
H2: Step 3 - Connect an Agent to a Managed MCP Server (DatabricksMCPClient, StreamableHTTP, resource listing)
H2: Step 4 - Unity Catalog Integration Deep Dive (Permission model, on-behalf-of-user auth, data access patterns per server type)
H2: Step 5 - Monitor and Govern with AI Gateway (Rate limits, access control, audit logging, MCP Catalog)
H2: Advanced: Building Custom MCP Servers with Databricks Apps (FastMCP + FastAPI, deploying to Databricks Apps)
H2: Managed vs Self-Hosted MCP: Making the Right Choice (Decision framework, cost and ops tradeoffs)
